prose:
Calling chemkit’s build-from-smiles skill (Open Babel 3D embed) with the name “acetate” produced a 7-atom structure resolved via PubChem (CID 175, isomeric SMILES “CC(=O)[O-]”).

Method / provenance
Property: 3D Cartesian geometry (.xyz) of the acetate anion
Level of theory / build tool: Open Babel ‑-gen3d coordinate embedding (no QM)
Geometry provenance: SMILES resolved from PubChem and converted to 3D coordinates with the chemkit build-from-smiles skill (single conformer; no further optimization)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (none; pure geometry build)
Charge and multiplicity: −1, singlet (multiplicity 1) inferred from SMILES
Software: Open Babel (via chemkit build-from-smiles)
Convergence / warnings: build completed; no engine warnings reported

Files: /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/build-validation/acetate/20260619-134521_acetate_build/agent_call_01.xyz contains the generated coordinates.

These coordinates are computationally generated; no experimental measurement is implied.
